name: pip-skills
description: Claude Code skills for serious engineering work. Architectural doctrine enforcement and PR review, built for real workflows and shared because they might help yours.

plugins.name: puritan
plugins.description: Architectural doctrine enforcement through composable lenses. Covenant for planning, Inquisition for auditing, Scriptorium for writing new doctrine.

plugins.name: tribunal
plugins.description: PR comment review, categorisation, prioritisation and resolution. No heresy goes unaddressed.

plugins.name: email-absolution
plugins.description: HTML email auditing and generation under the watchful eye of the Witchfinder. Twelve doctrines. Three skills. No sinful templates shall pass.

plugins.name: django-inquisition
plugins.description: Django ORM performance auditor. Audits source files and symbols against ~70 ORM heuristics, ranks findings by impact, and emits a tier-grouped report.

plugins.name: review-clone
plugins.description: Clone a reviewer's voice and rules from their public GitHub review history. Every finding cites a real comment; the persona never invents APIs.

plugins.name: overseer
plugins.description: Persistent work ledger plus card orchestration: delegated agents, adversarial review loops, budget tripwires and session handoff.

plugins.name: vigil
plugins.description: Portable context handover: measure a session's context usage (ctx NN%) and reset in-process via /clear at points you choose, resuming from a re-injected handover. Works in any repo; ships fail-safe Stop/SessionStart hooks.
